Fetch market data

Enter a ticker symbol. Data loads automatically from Stooq — no login required.

ⓘ Enter the ticker according to the formats given below
Ticker formats:
Indian NSE: TCS.NS  BSE: TCS.BO
US stocks: AAPL  Futures: BZ=F (Brent)  CL=F (WTI)
Indices: ^NSEI (NIFTY)  ^GSPC (S&P500)
How data fetching works (fully automatic): The app tries 7 sources in sequence — you will see live progress in the status bar below the Fetch button:

❶ Stooq direct  ·  ❷ Yahoo Finance direct  ·  ❸ Yahoo via corsproxy  ·  ❹ Yahoo via allorigins  ·  ❺ Yahoo query2 proxy  ·  ❻ Stooq via allorigins  ·  ❼ Stooq via corsproxy

Each attempt has a 10–12 second timeout. If all 7 fail, a yellow Upload CSV button appears — download the CSV from Yahoo Finance, Stooq, or Investing.com and upload it. The app reads all three formats automatically.
Months24
≈ 504 trading days of history

⚠ All automatic sources failed
Download the CSV manually from Yahoo Finance, then upload it here.
Go to finance.yahoo.com → search your ticker → Historical Data → Download CSV
🔒 Privacy Note
QuantSim Pro does not collect, store, or process any personal data. All computations are performed locally within your browser, and no user inputs or results are transmitted to any server.
We do not require login, do not track user activity, and do not use cookies for data collection purposes.
Any market data used within the platform is fetched from third-party public sources solely for simulation purposes.

Confirm base & currency

The simulation starts from the last closing price in your historical data. All parameters are auto-calculated. Just confirm the currency below.

Calculated parameters

All values are computed automatically from your historical data. You can fine-tune them if needed.

Computed from historical data
Base price S₀
–
last date in data
Annual drift μ
–
mean log-return × 252
Annual vol σ
–
std dev × √252
These values are derived from – trading days of historical price data. The simulation will project forward from S₀ using GBM with these calibrated parameters.
Fine-tune if needed
%
Mean daily log-return × 252. Positive = historically upward trend.
%
Std deviation of daily log-returns × √252. Drives the width of the simulation fan.
%
Auto-set based on detected currency. India: RBI repo ≈ 6.5% · US Fed ≈ 4.5% · EU ECB ≈ 3.5%

Scenarios

Define up to 4 scenarios. Each multiplies σ. Use ×1.0 for base, ×1.5 for stress.

Run simulation

Set horizon and path count, then launch.
